relevant_files.role: SecurityScanner orchestrating pattern matching on PR diffs
Integration tests with multi-line limitation documented

conventions.commits: Conventional commits, GPG signed, DCO sign-off required
conventions.testing: Unit tests in source files with #[cfg(test)], integration tests in tests/ directory, cargo test
conventions.linting: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -D warnings, actionlint for workflows
conventions.error_handling: anyhow::Result for application error handling, thiserror for library types

patterns: Regex-based line-by-line pattern matching via PatternEngine
JSON-encoded vulnerability patterns with file extension filters
Security scan triggered by PR labels and file path heuristics

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
Add negative test documenting SecurityScanner limitation
related_issues.relevance: Documents regex limitation for multi-line detection

constraints: Must maintain backwards compatibility with existing pattern JSON format
Should handle multiple languages (Rust, Python, JS, Java, Go)
Performance: PR scanning should remain under 5 seconds
Existing regex patterns should work alongside AST-based detection
test_coverage: Unit tests in patterns.rs cover 7 vulnerability types (API keys, SQL injection, path traversal, command injection, XSS, weak crypto). Integration tests in security_integration.rs verify detection against fixture files. Test test_multi_line_vulnerability_not_detected explicitly documents the known limitation.

library_findings.relevant_api: Parser, Language, Tree, Node - parse() returns Tree, cursor() for traversal
library_findings.notes: Cross-language AST parsing, requires language grammars

library_findings.relevant_api: Query, QueryCursor - for pattern matching on AST
library_findings.notes: Declarative pattern matching, useful for custom security rules

approaches.name: Minimal Hybrid (Recommended)
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter as optional dependency for specific patterns (SQL injection, command injection). Keep existing regex engine for single-line patterns. New AST engine returns findings in same Finding struct format.

approaches.name: Full AST Rewrite
approaches.description: Replace PatternEngine with tree-sitter-based AST engine. Convert all 14 patterns to tree-sitter-query rules. Remove patterns.json in favor of .scm query files.

approaches.name: Staged Migration with Feature Flags
approaches.description: Use cargo features to gradually migrate. Start with feature ast off by default. Run both engines in parallel, compare findings during migration period. Feature flag enables AST engine selectively.

recommendation: Minimal Hybrid approach is recommended. It addresses the core issue (multi-line vulnerability detection) while maintaining backwards compatibility and minimizing risk. The existing test in #736 documents the exact limitation this solves. Start with SQL injection and command injection patterns - these are the highest-value multi-line cases with clear AST structures. Benchmark performance before expanding to other patterns.
